2606.14570.title: Regional Climate Model Emulation with Diffusion Approaches: What is the Added Value of Generative Machine Learning?
2606.14570.paper_summary: Emulators provide a cost-effective alternative to regional climate models (RCMs) by capturing their dynamical downscaling function. They link large-scale predictors simulated by global climate models (GCMs) to RCM-simulated high-resolution fields of the target variable, here precipitation. Machine learning methods, typically deep learning, are cheaper than running RCMs in computation time and energy. Among them, generative models are appealing because they can simulate ensembles of local high-resolution fields consistent with the predictors. This ensemble, which we call the uncertainty envelope, remains to be properly assessed for added value. Here, we make three contributions. First, we introduce ParamDiffusion, a new two-stage diffusion-based framework, and compare it with a state-of-the-art diffusion approach. Second, we expand standard validation through a comprehensive framework aligned with climate-science needs, examining specific precipitation events, including extremes. Third, within this framework, we assess the added value of diffusion approaches relative to deterministic methods. We intercompare four deep-learning models: a deterministic model designed to capture the precipitation tail; a parametric probabilistic model based on it; a recently proposed diffusion approach; and ParamDiffusion, which couples the parametric model with a diffusion model. Our results show that diffusion-based approaches reproduce climatological precipitation statistics with high skill, including distributional tails and spatially compounded extremes, while generating spatially detailed fields. However, none of the assessed models consistently accounts for the most extreme RCM-simulated events within its uncertainty envelope. Diffusion models are therefore promising for probabilistic RCM emulation, but progress is still required before they can reliably represent high-impact precipitation extremes.
2606.14570.paper_summary_zh: 模擬器透過捕捉其動態下縮功能，提供了一種成本效益高的區域氣候模型（RCMs）替代方案。它們將全球氣候模型（GCMs）模擬的大尺度預測因子與RCM模擬的目標變數（此處為降水）的高解析度場連結起來。機器學習方法，通常是深度學習，在計算時間和能量上比運行RCMs更便宜。在這些方法中，生成模型因能夠模擬與預測因子一致的本地高解析度場的集合而具有吸引力。這個集合，我們稱之為不確定性包絡，仍需進行適當評估以確認其附加價值。在此，我們做出三項貢獻。首先，我們介紹ParamDiffusion，一種新的兩階段基於擴散的框架，並將其與最先進的擴散方法進行比較。其次，我們通過一個與氣候科學需求相一致的綜合框架擴展標準驗證，檢查特定的降水事件，包括極端事件。第三，在這個框架內，我們評估擴散方法相對於確定性方法的附加價值。我們互相比較四個深度學習模型：一個設計用於捕捉降水尾部的確定性模型；一個基於它的參數概率模型；一種最近提出的擴散方法；以及ParamDiffusion，將參數模型與擴散模型結合。我们的结果表明，基于扩散的方法以高技能再现气候学降水统计，包括分布尾部和空间复合极端，同时生成空间详细的场。然而，所评估的模型中没有一个能在其不确定性包络内持续考虑最极端的RCM模拟事件。因此，扩散模型在概率RCM仿真方面是有前景的，但在它们能够可靠地表示高影响降水极端之前，仍需取得进展。
2606.14570.author: Mikel N. Legasa et.al.
2606.14570.authors: Mikel N. Legasa, Antoine Doury, Achille Gellens, Redouane Lguensat, Clara Naldesi, Soulivanh Thao, Mathieu Vrac
